title: Cyclosorus exindusiatus
intro: Cyclosorus exindusiatus, also known as the Chinese leopards' bane or the Chinese fern, is a species of fern in the family Woodsiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus exindusiatus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cyclosorus exindusiatus is commonly prepared as a tea or a tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to promote health and well-being.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus exindusiatus is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns. This species is further classified under the genus Cyclosorus, which belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The classification of Cyclosorus exindusiatus is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, Family: Thelypteridaceae, Genus: Cyclosorus, Species: C. exindusiatus. Cyclosorus exindusiatus is native to various regions, including Asia and Australia. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but the above classification is widely accepted.
